mechanisms.description: All requests authenticated via API-Token header
mechanisms.notes: Each user has unique API key. Deleting user breaks integrations using that key. No OAuth option available — API key only.

authentication.notes: API key can be reset from Developer settings if compromised

rate_limits.not_documented: ActiveCampaign does not publicly document specific rate limits
rate_limits.notes: Contact support or account manager for specific rate limit details. 429 errors indicate rate limiting.

webhooks.description: Webhooks available for various events (contact created, deal updated, etc.)

error_dictionary.description: Malformed request body or missing required parameters
error_dictionary.example_real: POST /api/3/contactTags with body {"contact":"1","tag":"2"} → returns 'No tag id provided'
error_dictionary.real_response: {"errors":[{"title":"Invalid value for query parameter contactTag.tag","detail":"No tag id provided","source":{"parameter":"contactTag.tag"}}]}
common_causes: Wrapping payload incorrectly (e.g., {contact, tag} instead of {contactTag: {contact, tag}})
error_dictionary.solution: Always wrap resource-specific payloads in a nested object named after the resource. For contactTags: {"contactTag":{"contact":"1","tag":"2"}}. Read API docs carefully for expected JSON structure

error_dictionary.real_response: {"message":"No Result found for CustomerAccount with id 2"}
error_dictionary.solution: Verify Api-Token header is present and correct. Reset API key from Developer settings if compromised

error_dictionary.description: Account payment issues — subscription expired or billing problem
error_dictionary.solution: Check account billing status in ActiveCampaign settings

error_dictionary.description: Authentication failed or user not authorized for resource
causes: API key recycled without updating client
User removed from account but key still being used
error_dictionary.solution: Ensure API key is valid and user has appropriate permissions. Regenerate key if needed and update all integrations
Check resource ID, verify the endpoint path, confirm account has the resource (list, tag, etc.)

error_dictionary.description: Missing or invalid parameter — request understood but cannot be processed
error_dictionary.real_response: {"errors":[{"title":"The connection service was not provided."},{"title":"The connection externalid was not provided."}]}
error_dictionary.solution: Check error titles for which fields are missing or invalid. Ensure all required parameters are provided with correct types

error_dictionary.description: Rate limit exceeded — sent too many requests in a given amount of time
error_dictionary.solution: Reduce request frequency. Contact support or account manager for rate limit details and possible increases

community_constraints.issue: contactTags endpoint has confusing nested JSON requirement
community_constraints.detail: Many developers try {"contact":"1","tag":"2"} but API expects {"contactTag":{"contact":"1","tag":"2"}}. Docs bug — example shows wrong format.
community_constraints.workaround: Always nest resource data under the resource name key. For contactTags: use contactTag wrapper. For contacts: use contact wrapper, etc.

community_constraints.issue: 403 Forbidden when API key is recycled — Python library crashes with KeyError
community_constraints.detail: When API returns 403, the Python wrapper library tries to read response.headers['Content-Type'] which may be missing, causing KeyError instead of a proper error message
community_constraints.workaround: Always handle HTTP errors explicitly. Check response status code before parsing body. Patch: wrap in try/except or use a more robust library

community_constraints.issue: HTTP errors not properly reported by official wrappers
community_constraints.detail: ActiveCampaign API often responds with error statuses. Library currently hides them and returns error body without raising exceptions
community_constraints.workaround: Implement custom error handling that checks response.status_code and raises exceptions for 4xx/5xx. Do not rely on library to handle errors gracefully

community_constraints.issue: No OAuth — API key only, which ties to individual user accounts
community_constraints.detail: ActiveCampaign only supports API key authentication (no OAuth). Each key is tied to a specific user. If user leaves, all integrations using their key break.
community_constraints.workaround: Use a dedicated service account (not a real person) for API integrations. Regularly audit which users own API keys. Document key-to-integration mapping

use_cases: Sync contacts from CRM to ActiveCampaign for email automation
Tag contacts based on CRM lifecycle stages to trigger automations
Create deals in ActiveCampaign from form submissions or external events
Sync account/company data bidirectionally with external systems
Automate contact list management based on behavioral triggers from other platforms
